title: Logging should not be vulnerable to injection attacks
description: Log injection occurs when an application fails to sanitize untrusted data used for logging. An attacker can forge log content to prevent an organization from being able to trace back malicious activities. What is the potential impact? If an attacker can insert arbitrary data into a log file, the integrity of the chain of events being recorded can be compromised.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.triggering_conditions: Logging method call with single variable argument (no template or literal)
never_trigger_on: Structured logging with templates (e.g., LogInformation("Message {Param}", value))
logic.reasoning: Detects logging calls where user input or variables are passed directly as the only argument without using structured logging templates. This prevents log injection attacks where malicious users can forge log entries. The pattern matches logging methods followed by a single variable name in parentheses, excluding cases with string literals (quotes), templates (curly braces with comma), string interpolation ($), test code, and comments.
